summary.intent: Create a feature branch for 'entire' tool integration changes, commit them, open a PR, then watch PR #37 until it merges and clean up the branch automatically.
summary.outcome: Feature branch `add-entire-integration` was created, changes committed, PR #37 opened and merged, main updated via fast-forward to commit `9a83a41`, and both local and remote branches cleaned up.

repo: Repository is at fenv-org/mementor on GitHub, owner account is powdream
Project uses Rust with pre-commit hooks that run rustfmt and clippy checks
GitHub auto-deletes remote branches on PR merge for this repo
.entire/ directory uses .gitignore to exclude most of its contents, only tracking .gitignore and settings.json

code.finding: Integrates 'entire' tool via hooks: SessionStart, SessionEnd, Stop, PreToolUse, PostToolUse events added, plus deny permissions and hook reordering
New file introduced as part of entire tool integration configuration
New file that gates what inside .entire/ gets tracked by git

workflow: Polling `gh pr view --json state` is a viable pattern for watching PR merge status in a script loop
After a PR merge, `git fetch origin` + `git merge --ff-only` is the clean way to update main
Local branch deletion with `git branch -d` is safe post-merge; remote cleanup is handled automatically by GitHub for this repo
